key_insight: HVAF spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that retain martensitic transformation and shape-memory functionality after post-annealing, despite a heterogeneous as-sprayed microstructure.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical route to functional NiTi surface layers for wear, friction, corrosion, and thermal-actuation applications on steel components.
method: HVAF sprayed NiTi onto mild steel using four processing parameter sets
Characterized composition, porosity, microstructure, phase transformation, and thermomechanical response
Compared as-sprayed and annealed behavior using bending, nanoindentation, and scratch tests
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 100–300 μm coatings that underwent martensitic transformation and showed thermal actuation and shape-memory effects in mechanical tests.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings are microstructurally inhomogeneous with oxides, voids, and residual stress, so annealing and adhesion/strength limits must be considered before deployment.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
